findings.evidence: `rg` shows the declaration at `DiagonalizableGroupFunctoriality.lean:69`, while the relevant API lives in Mathlib under `namespace MonoidAlgebra`: `RingTheory/Bialgebra/MonoidAlgebra.lean:68` defines `mapDomainBialgHom` and `Algebra/MonoidAlgebra/MapDomain.lean:57` proves `mapDomain_single`.
findings.fix: Make this lemma `private` if it is only a local proof helper, or move it to a generic `MonoidAlgebra`/bialgebra home under the `MonoidAlgebra` namespace.
findings.issue: `TauCeti.DiagonalizableGroup.mapDomainBialgHom_single_one` is not diagonalizable-group API; it is a generic statement about `MonoidAlgebra.mapDomainBialgHom` on `single g 1`, so exporting it from `DiagonalizableGroup` misplaces reusable monoid-algebra material.

summary: One public helper is placed in the diagonalizable-group namespace even though it is a generic monoid-algebra fact. The diagonalizable functoriality declarations themselves are in a natural file with direct imports.
